edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:b8c9c7b38d00
edges.original_sentence: Within five years of wastewater addition to the marsh, over 130 bird species had been reintroduced, several other species of flora and fauna had reestablished, and the marsh had demonstrated a significant capacity for wastewater nutrient reduction.

source_spans.text: Within five years of wastewater addition to the marsh, over 130 bird species had been reintroduced, several other species of flora and fauna had reestablished, and the marsh had demonstrated a significant capacity for wastewater nutrient reduction.

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:b8c9c7b38d00
edges.original_sentence: Within five years of wastewater addition to the marsh, over 130 bird species had been reintroduced, several other species of flora and fauna had reestablished, and the marsh had demonstrated a significant capacity for wastewater nutrient reduction.

source_spans.text: Within five years of wastewater addition to the marsh, over 130 bird species had been reintroduced, several other species of flora and fauna had reestablished, and the marsh had demonstrated a significant capacity for wastewater nutrient reduction.

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:b2c0306ce67f
edges.original_sentence: Agricultural drainage and encroachment had substantially reduced the size of Frank Lake and by the early 1980s, the wetland was dry.

source_spans.text: Agricultural drainage and encroachment had substantially reduced the size of Frank Lake and by the early 1980s, the wetland was dry.

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:0ca5ed74d750
edges.original_sentence: Disturbance by cultivation, repeated heavy grazing, and fire suppression had reduced or eliminated several native plant and animal populations (Wallis and others 1996), such as the extirpation of wolf (Canis lupus), coyote (Canis latrans), badger (Taxidea taxus), swift fox (Vulpes velox), antelope (Antilocapra americana ), sharp-tailed grouse ( Tympanuchus phasianellus), and greater prairie chicken (Tympanuchus cupido) from the High River area (Fowler 1937).

source_spans.text: Disturbance by cultivation, repeated heavy grazing, and fire suppression had reduced or eliminated several native plant and animal populations (Wallis and others 1996), such as the extirpation of wolf (Canis lupus), coyote (Canis latrans), badger (Taxidea taxus), swift fox (Vulpes velox), antelope (Antilocapra americana ), sharp-tailed grouse ( Tympanuchus phasianellus), and greater prairie chicken (Tympanuchus cupido) from the High River area (Fowler 1937).

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:c981f5dbf3c0
edges.original_sentence: Many avian species have only recently returned to Frank Lake following marsh restoration in 1990, including 44 waterfowl species (Table 1), 15 raptor species (Table 2), 50 shorebird species (Table 3), and 28 other bird species (Table 4).

source_spans.text: Many avian species have only recently returned to Frank Lake following marsh restoration in 1990, including 44 waterfowl species (Table 1), 15 raptor species (Table 2), 50 shorebird species (Table 3), and 28 other bird species (Table 4).

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:95c209c7c0fc
edges.original_sentence: The success of upland and water management strategies is reflected in the increase of target species’ abundance and richness: 50 shorebird species, 44 waterfowl species, 15 raptor species, and 28 other new bird species have returned to the marsh since restoration.

source_spans.text: The success of upland and water management strategies is reflected in the increase of target species’ abundance and richness: 50 shorebird species, 44 waterfowl species, 15 raptor species, and 28 other new bird species have returned to the marsh since restoration.

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:ad49ff2feb0c
edges.original_sentence: As well, significant N and P reduction occurs as waters flow through the first basin of the marsh.

source_spans.text: As well, significant N and P reduction occurs as waters flow through the first basin of the marsh.

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:02ac5ff2dd03
edges.original_sentence: For the beef-processing facility (Cargill Foods Ltd.), the creation of an industry and a wetland for wastewater treatment that could treat industrial wastewater and improve water quality in nearby watercourses was both profitable and environmentally sound.

source_spans.text: For the beef-processing facility (Cargill Foods Ltd.), the creation of an industry and a wetland for wastewater treatment that could treat industrial wastewater and improve water quality in nearby watercourses was both profitable and environmentally sound.

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:6cd9649c0452
edges.original_sentence: In addition to creating both upland and wetland habitats, the project eliminated municipal wastewater dumping into the Highwood River, quickly improving water quality in the Highwood River (Sosiak personal communication) and reducing stress on the famous trout fishery.

source_spans.text: In addition to creating both upland and wetland habitats, the project eliminated municipal wastewater dumping into the Highwood River, quickly improving water quality in the Highwood River (Sosiak personal communication) and reducing stress on the famous trout fishery.

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:4f9e87224e65
edges.original_sentence: Results from local plant and animal inventories show that this cooperative venture has been quite successful in restoring site biodiversity (Wallis and others 1996).

source_spans.text: Results from local plant and animal inventories show that this cooperative venture has been quite successful in restoring site biodiversity (Wallis and others 1996).

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:d68878382793
edges.original_sentence: Almost 800 ha of upland cover have already been restored to native grasses and shrubs by seeding (Sadler and others 1995).

source_spans.text: Almost 800 ha of upland cover have already been restored to native grasses and shrubs by seeding (Sadler and others 1995).

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:558a2a72178f
edges.original_sentence: An additional 283 ha (700 acres) of marsh is added to the existing basin every spring at Frank Lake with backflood irrigation (Haworth-Brockman and Smallwood 1989).

source_spans.text: An additional 283 ha (700 acres) of marsh is added to the existing basin every spring at Frank Lake with backflood irrigation (Haworth-Brockman and Smallwood 1989).

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:ba32b2e68708
edges.original_sentence: The success of the nesting structures is reflected in the large number of immature birds banded at Frank Lake from 1993 to 1996 (Table 8).

source_spans.text: The success of the nesting structures is reflected in the large number of immature birds banded at Frank Lake from 1993 to 1996 (Table 8).

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:0e854e09a53a
edges.original_sentence: Despite the short-circuiting of marsh water, preliminary findings of nutrient treatment by the marsh showed total phosphorus retention of 64% and NH3 retention of 87% during 1994–1995 (White and Bayley unpublished).

source_spans.text: Despite the short-circuiting of marsh water, preliminary findings of nutrient treatment by the marsh showed total phosphorus retention of 64% and NH3 retention of 87% during 1994–1995 (White and Bayley unpublished).

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:d43d3e029a48
edges.original_sentence: Management and recreation activities, such as hunting and bird-watching are minimized at Frank Lake during critical periods to reduce impacts on wildlife, and trail creation has been curbed in sensitive and productive areas.

source_spans.text: Management and recreation activities, such as hunting and bird-watching are minimized at Frank Lake during critical periods to reduce impacts on wildlife, and trail creation has been curbed in sensitive and productive areas.

source_document.title: Restoration of a Canadian Prairie Wetland with Agricultural and Municipal Wastewater
